text: Genetic and Rare Diseases Information Center | GARD What is the Genetic and Rare Diseases Information Center? Progress in data science and an increased understanding of disease genetics lead experts to agree that more than an estimated 10,000 rare diseases affect millions of people in the United States.
source: Genetic and Rare Diseases (GARD) Information Center

text: Each disease may be rare individually, but people with rare diseases often face similar challenges. These challenges may include accessing information, getting a diagnosis, and finding resources. The mission of the Genetic and Rare Diseases (GARD) Information Center is to help the rare disease community meet these common challenges.
source: Genetic and Rare Diseases (GARD) Information Center

text: We do this by providing free access to reliable and easy-to-understand information. We support the rare disease community both through our website and through the dedicated Information Specialists in our Contact Center. Finding accurate information and helpful resources may prove challenging during your diagnostic journey.
source: Genetic and Rare Diseases (GARD) Information Center

text: GARD provides access to information and resources that are current, reliable, and clear. Using the support GARD offers can help ensure you are getting the best possible care for your rare disease. Here, learn what kind of information and resources GARD offers. Search our database to find information about thousands of rare diseases.
source: Genetic and Rare Diseases (GARD) Information Center

text: Reach one of our Information Specialists by email or phone to find the help you may need. Discover everything from where to find financial aid to tools that help you communicate with your health care provider. Connect with other patients and families to build a community of support, learn about the latest treatments, care centers, and opportunities to participate in research.
source: Genetic and Rare Diseases (GARD) Information Center

text: GARD Information Specialists offer individualized help to connect you with information and resources. Our Information Specialists can help you: Find or understand information about a rare disease. Navigate information throughout the diagnostic journey. Discover resources, disease experts, or clinical studies for a rare disease.
source: Genetic and Rare Diseases (GARD) Information Center

text: Important: GARD provides information about rare diseases for your general knowledge and is not a substitute for the advice of a health care provider. Leave a detailed voice message and a GARD information specialist will respond to your inquiry. Use the contact form to send your question to a GARD Information Specialist. Please allow 2 to 10 business days for us to respond.
source: Genetic and Rare Diseases (GARD) Information Center
